query: You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository. The codebase is at version 2.174 (commit: 6d2f8951). ## Issue to Resolve [BUG]: Answer comparison not shown when type answer into the card is enabled ### Checked for duplicates? - [X] This issue is not a duplicate ### What are the steps to reproduce this bug? - Enable `Advanced â†’ Type answer into the card` - Review a card that requires typing the answer. ### Expected behaviour After clicking "Show Answer", the comparision of the typed and actual answers should be shown. It is the case when the above-mentioned Advanced option is disabled. ### Actual behaviour Only the correct answer is shown. ### Debug info ```text 2.17 beta 4 ``` ### (Optional) Anything else you want to share? This might be a long-standing bug. ### Research - [X] I am reporting a bug specific to AnkiDroid (Android app) - [X] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue - [X] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting)) ## Relevant Files The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue: - `AnkiDroid/src/main/assets/scripts/card.js` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/AbstractFlashcardViewer.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/cardviewer/TypeAnswer.kt` ## Instructions Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above. Focus on: - Understanding the root cause of the issue - Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem - Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality ## Important Evaluation Notes **Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing. **If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files ## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS **NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

If not, see . 15â†’ */ 16â†’ 17â†’package com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer 18â†’ 19â†’import android.content.SharedPreferences 20â†’import android.content.res.Resources 21â†’import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting 22â†’import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager 23â†’import com.ichi2.anki.R 24â†’import com.ichi2.anki.servicelayer.LanguageHint 25â†’import com.ichi2.anki.servicelayer.LanguageHintService 26â†’import com.ichi2.libanki.Card 27â†’import com.ichi2.libanki.Collection 28â†’import com.ichi2.utils.jsonObjectIterable 29â†’import org.intellij.lang.annotations.Language 30â†’import org.json.JSONArray 31â†’import timber.log.Timber 32â†’import java.util.regex.Matcher 33â†’import java.util.regex.Pattern 34â†’ 35â†’// TODO: investigate whether it's feasible to drop the useInputTag option introduced back 36â†’// in 2015. https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/pull/3921 37â†’ 38â†’class TypeAnswer( 39â†’ @get:JvmName("useInputTag") val useInputTag: Boolean, 40â†’ @get:JvmName("doNotUseCodeFormatting") val doNotUseCodeFormatting: Boolean, 41â†’ /** Preference: Whether the user wants to focus "type in answer" */ 42â†’ val autoFocus: Boolean 43â†’) { 44â†’ 45â†’ /** The correct answer in the compare to field if answer should be given by learner. Null if no answer is expected. */ 46â†’ var correct: String? = null 47â†’ private set 48â†’ 49â†’ /** What the learner actually typed (externally mutable) */ 50â†’ var input = "" 51â†’ 52â†’ /** Font face of the 'compare to' field */ 53â†’ var font = "" 54â†’ private set 55â†’ 56â†’ /** The font size of the 'compare to' field */ 57â†’ var size = 0 58â†’ private set 59â†’ 60â†’ var languageHint: LanguageHint? = null 61â†’ private set 62â†’ 63â†’ /** 64â†’ * Optional warning for when a typed answer can't be displayed 65â†’ * 66â†’ * * empty card [R.string.empty_card_warning] 67â†’ * * unknown field specified [R.string.unknown_type_field_warning] 68â†’ * */ 69â†’ var warning: String? = null 70â†’ private set 71â†’ 72â†’ /** 73â†’ * @return true If entering input via EditText 74â†’ * and if the current card has a {{type:field}} on the card template 75â†’ */ 76â†’ fun validForEditText(): Boolean { 77â†’ return !useInputTag && correct != null 78â†’ } 79â†’ 80â†’ fun autoFocusEditText(): Boolean { 81â†’ return validForEditText() && autoFocus 82â†’ } 83â†’ 84â†’ /** 85â†’ * Extract type answer/cloze text and font/size 86â†’ * @param card The next card to display 87â†’ */ 88â†’ fun updateInfo(col: Collection, card: Card, res: Resources) { 89â†’ correct = null 90â†’ val q = card.question(col) 91â†’ val m = PATTERN.matcher(q) 92â†’ var clozeIdx = 0 93â†’ if (!m.find()) { 94â†’ return 95â†’ } 96â†’ var fldTag = m.group(1)!! 97â†’ // if it's a cloze, extract data 98â†’ if (fldTag.startsWith("cloze:")) { 99â†’ // get field and cloze position 100â†’ clozeIdx = card.ord + 1 101â†’ fldTag = fldTag.split(":").toTypedArray()[1] 102â†’ } 103â†’ // loop through fields for a match 104â†’ val flds: JSONArray = card.model(col).getJSONArray("flds") 105â†’ for (fld in flds.jsonObjectIterable()) { 106â†’ val name = fld.getString("name") 107â†’ if (name == fldTag) { 108â†’ correct = card.note(col).getItem(name) 109â†’ if (clozeIdx != 0) { 110â†’ // narrow to cloze 111â†’ correct = contentForCloze(correct!!, clozeIdx) 112â†’ } 113â†’ font = fld.getString("font") 114â†’ size = fld.getInt("size") 115â†’ languageHint = LanguageHintService.getLanguageHintForField(fld) 116â†’ break 117â†’ } 118â†’ } 119â†’ when (correct) { 120â†’ null -> { 121â†’ warning = if (clozeIdx != 0) { 122â†’ res.getString(R.string.empty_card_warning) 123â†’ } else { 124â†’ res.getString(R.string.unknown_type_field_warning, fldTag) 125â†’ } 126â†’ } 127â†’ "" -> { 128â†’ correct = null 129â†’ } 130â†’ else -> { 131â†’ warning = null 132â†’ } 133â†’ } 134â†’ } 135â†’ 136â†’ /** 137â†’ * Format question field when it contains typeAnswer or clozes. If there was an error during type text extraction, a 138â†’ * warning is displayed 139â†’ * 140â†’ * @param buf The question text 141â†’ * @return The formatted question text 142â†’ */ 143â†’ fun filterQuestion(buf: String): String { 144â†’ val m = PATTERN.matcher(buf) 145â†’ if (warning != null) { 146â†’ return m.replaceFirst(warning!!) 147â†’ } 148â†’ val sb = java.lang.StringBuilder() 149â†’ fun append(@Language("HTML") html: String) = sb.append(html) 150â†’ if (useInputTag) { 151â†’ // These functions are defined in the JavaScript file assets/scripts/card.js. We get the text back in 152â†’ // shouldOverrideUrlLoading() in createWebView() in this file. 153â†’ append( 154â†’ """
155â†’ 0) { 160â†’ append("style=\"font-family: '").append(font).append("'; font-size: ") 161â†’ .append(size).append("px;\" ") 162â†’ } 163â†’ append(">\n
\n") 164â†’ } else { 165â†’ append("........") 167â†’ } 168â†’ return m.replaceAll(sb.toString()) 169â†’ } 170â†’ 171â†’ /** 172â†’ * Fill the placeholder for the type comparison: `[[type:(.+?)]]` 173â†’ * 174â†’ * Replaces with the HTML for the correct answer, and the comparison to the correct answer if appropriate. 175â†’ * 176â†’ * @param answer The card content on the back of the card 177â†’ * 178â†’ * @return The formatted answer text with `[[type:(.+?)]]` replaced with HTML 179â†’ */ 180â†’ fun filterAnswer(answer: String): String { 181â†’ val userAnswer = input 182â†’ val correctAnswer = correct 183â†’ Timber.d("correct answer = %s", correctAnswer) 184â†’ Timber.d("user answer = %s", userAnswer) 185â†’ return filterAnswer(answer, userAnswer, correctAnswer ?: "") 186â†’ } 187â†’ 188â†’ /** 189â†’ * Fill the placeholder for the type comparison. Show the correct answer, and the comparison if appropriate. 190â†’ * 191â†’ * @param answer The answer text 192â†’ * @param userAnswer Text typed by the user, or empty. 193â†’ * @param correctAnswer The correct answer, taken from the note. 194â†’ * @return The formatted answer text 195â†’ */ 196â†’ fun filterAnswer(answer: String, userAnswer: String, correctAnswer: String): String { 197â†’ val m: Matcher = PATTERN.matcher(answer) 198â†’ val sb = StringBuilder() 199â†’ fun append(@Language("HTML") html: String) = sb.append(html) 200â†’ 201â†’ var comparisonText = CollectionManager.compareAnswer(correctAnswer, userAnswer) 202â†’ if (doNotUseCodeFormatting) { 203â†’ comparisonText = "$comparisonText" 204â†’ } 205â†’ append(Matcher.quoteReplacement(comparisonText)) 206â†’ return m.replaceAll(sb.toString()) 207â†’ } 208â†’ 209â†’ companion object { 210â†’ /** Regular expression in card data for a 'type answer' after processing has occurred */ 211â†’ val PATTERN: Pattern = Pattern.compile("\\[\\[type:(.+?)]]") 212â†’ 213â†’ fun createInstance(preferences: SharedPreferences): TypeAnswer { 214â†’ return TypeAnswer( 215â†’ useInputTag = preferences.getBoolean("useInputTag", false), 216â†’ doNotUseCodeFormatting = preferences.getBoolean("noCodeFormatting", false), 217â†’ autoFocus = preferences.getBoolean("autoFocusTypeInAnswer", true) 218â†’ ) 219â†’ } 220â†’ 221â†’ /** 222â†’ * Return the correct answer to use for {{type::cloze::NN}} fields. 223â†’ * 224â†’ * @param txt The field text with the clozes 225â†’ * @param idx The index of the cloze to use 226â†’ * @return If the cloze strings are the same, return a single cloze string, otherwise, return 227â†’ * a string with a comma-separeted list of strings with the correct index. 228â†’ */ 229â†’ @VisibleForTesting 230â†’ fun contentForCloze(txt: String, idx: Int): String? { 231â†’ // In Android, } should be escaped 232â†’ val re = Pattern.compile("\\{\\{c$idx::(.+?)\\}\\}") 233â†’ val m = re.matcher(txt) 234â†’ val matches: MutableList = ArrayList() 235â†’ var groupOne: String 236â†’ while (m.find()) { 237â†’ groupOne = m.group(1)!! 238â†’ val colonColonIndex = groupOne.indexOf("::") 239â†’ if (colonColonIndex > -1) { 240â†’ // Cut out the hint. 241â†’ groupOne = groupOne.substring(0, colonColonIndex) 242â†’ } 243â†’ matches.add(groupOne) 244â†’ } 245â†’ val uniqMatches: Set = HashSet(matches) // Allow to check whether there are distinct strings 246â†’ 247â†’ // Make it consistent with the Desktop version (see issue #8229) 248â†’ return if (uniqMatches.size == 1) { 249â†’ matches[0] 250â†’ } else { 251â†’ matches.joinToString(", ") 252â†’ } 253â†’ } 254â†’ } 255â†’} 256â†’

content.thinking: I'm wondering if clicking the native answer causes the webview to lose focus. Iâ€™m not entirely sure, but it seems like the focus might still be on the web element, even if the view is removed, causing the input not to transfer properly. When I show the answer, the input may appear as empty, so I want to check if `compareAnswer` is just validating the correct answer without comparisons when there's no user answer provided. I'll look into how `CollectionManager.compareAnswer` is used.

content.thinking:
**Addressing answer comparison issue**

I'm looking into how the `compareAnswer` function works, as it seems to return HTML with correct and diff values when there are inputs. There's a bug where comparisons don't show up when users type directly in the card while using `useInputTag`. It seems that the input isn't captured unless the user interacts with it in a certain way. To fix this, I need to retrieve the current input value using a JavaScript interface before displaying the answer. I'll adjust the process to ensure the comparison is available as intended.**Considering making it async**

I'm thinking that we could switch to an asynchronous approach. It might involve calling `processCardAction` using the WebView instance. There's already a `processCardAction` method from earlier, which possibly works like `processCardAction { webView -> webView.evaluateJavascript(...) }`. I need to investigate how WebView is being utilized and look for the `evaluateJavascript` function in `AbstractFlashcardViewer`. This might help clarify how to implement the changes effectively.

If not, see . 15â†’ */ 16â†’ 17â†’package com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer 18â†’ 19â†’import android.content.SharedPreferences 20â†’import android.content.res.Resources 21â†’import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting 22â†’import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager 23â†’import com.ichi2.anki.R 24â†’import com.ichi2.anki.servicelayer.LanguageHint 25â†’import com.ichi2.anki.servicelayer.LanguageHintService 26â†’import com.ichi2.libanki.Card 27â†’import com.ichi2.libanki.Collection 28â†’import com.ichi2.utils.jsonObjectIterable 29â†’import org.intellij.lang.annotations.Language 30â†’import org.json.JSONArray 31â†’import timber.log.Timber 32â†’import java.util.regex.Matcher 33â†’import java.util.regex.Pattern 34â†’ 35â†’// TODO: investigate whether it's feasible to drop the useInputTag option introduced back 36â†’// in 2015. https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/pull/3921 37â†’ 38â†’class TypeAnswer( 39â†’ @get:JvmName("useInputTag") val useInputTag: Boolean, 40â†’ @get:JvmName("doNotUseCodeFormatting") val doNotUseCodeFormatting: Boolean, 41â†’ /** Preference: Whether the user wants to focus "type in answer" */ 42â†’ val autoFocus: Boolean 43â†’) { 44â†’ 45â†’ /** The correct answer in the compare to field if answer should be given by learner. Null if no answer is expected. */ 46â†’ var correct: String? = null 47â†’ private set 48â†’ 49â†’ /** What the learner actually typed (externally mutable) */ 50â†’ var input = "" 51â†’ 52â†’ /** Font face of the 'compare to' field */ 53â†’ var font = "" 54â†’ private set 55â†’ 56â†’ /** The font size of the 'compare to' field */ 57â†’ var size = 0 58â†’ private set 59â†’ 60â†’ var languageHint: LanguageHint? = null 61â†’ private set 62â†’ 63â†’ /** 64â†’ * Optional warning for when a typed answer can't be displayed 65â†’ * 66â†’ * * empty card [R.string.empty_card_warning] 67â†’ * * unknown field specified [R.string.unknown_type_field_warning] 68â†’ * */ 69â†’ var warning: String? = null 70â†’ private set 71â†’ 72â†’ /** 73â†’ * @return true If entering input via EditText 74â†’ * and if the current card has a {{type:field}} on the card template 75â†’ */ 76â†’ fun validForEditText(): Boolean { 77â†’ return !useInputTag && correct != null 78â†’ } 79â†’ 80â†’ fun autoFocusEditText(): Boolean { 81â†’ return validForEditText() && autoFocus 82â†’ } 83â†’ 84â†’ /** 85â†’ * Extract type answer/cloze text and font/size 86â†’ * @param card The next card to display 87â†’ */ 88â†’ fun updateInfo(col: Collection, card: Card, res: Resources) { 89â†’ correct = null 90â†’ val q = card.question(col) 91â†’ val m = PATTERN.matcher(q) 92â†’ var clozeIdx = 0 93â†’ if (!m.find()) { 94â†’ return 95â†’ } 96â†’ var fldTag = m.group(1)!! 97â†’ // if it's a cloze, extract data 98â†’ if (fldTag.startsWith("cloze:")) { 99â†’ // get field and cloze position 100â†’ clozeIdx = card.ord + 1 101â†’ fldTag = fldTag.split(":").toTypedArray()[1] 102â†’ } 103â†’ // loop through fields for a match 104â†’ val flds: JSONArray = card.model(col).getJSONArray("flds") 105â†’ for (fld in flds.jsonObjectIterable()) { 106â†’ val name = fld.getString("name") 107â†’ if (name == fldTag) { 108â†’ correct = card.note(col).getItem(name) 109â†’ if (clozeIdx != 0) { 110â†’ // narrow to cloze 111â†’ correct = contentForCloze(correct!!, clozeIdx) 112â†’ } 113â†’ font = fld.getString("font") 114â†’ size = fld.getInt("size") 115â†’ languageHint = LanguageHintService.getLanguageHintForField(fld) 116â†’ break 117â†’ } 118â†’ } 119â†’ when (correct) { 120â†’ null -> { 121â†’ warning = if (clozeIdx != 0) { 122â†’ res.getString(R.string.empty_card_warning) 123â†’ } else { 124â†’ res.getString(R.string.unknown_type_field_warning, fldTag) 125â†’ } 126â†’ } 127â†’ "" -> { 128â†’ correct = null 129â†’ } 130â†’ else -> { 131â†’ warning = null 132â†’ } 133â†’ } 134â†’ } 135â†’ 136â†’ /** 137â†’ * Format question field when it contains typeAnswer or clozes. If there was an error during type text extraction, a 138â†’ * warning is displayed 139â†’ * 140â†’ * @param buf The question text 141â†’ * @return The formatted question text 142â†’ */ 143â†’ fun filterQuestion(buf: String): String { 144â†’ val m = PATTERN.matcher(buf) 145â†’ if (warning != null) { 146â†’ return m.replaceFirst(warning!!) 147â†’ } 148â†’ val sb = java.lang.StringBuilder() 149â†’ fun append(@Language("HTML") html: String) = sb.append(html) 150â†’ if (useInputTag) { 151â†’ // These functions are defined in the JavaScript file assets/scripts/card.js. We get the text back in 152â†’ // shouldOverrideUrlLoading() in createWebView() in this file. 153â†’ append( 154â†’ """
155â†’ 0) { 160â†’ append("style=\"font-family: '").append(font).append("'; font-size: ") 161â†’ .append(size).append("px;\" ") 162â†’ } 163â†’ append(">\n
\n") 164â†’ } else { 165â†’ append("........") 167â†’ } 168â†’ return m.replaceAll(sb.toString()) 169â†’ } 170â†’ 171â†’ /** 172â†’ * Fill the placeholder for the type comparison: `[[type:(.+?)]]` 173â†’ * 174â†’ * Replaces with the HTML for the correct answer, and the comparison to the correct answer if appropriate. 175â†’ * 176â†’ * @param answer The card content on the back of the card 177â†’ * 178â†’ * @return The formatted answer text with `[[type:(.+?)]]` replaced with HTML 179â†’ */ 180â†’ fun filterAnswer(answer: String): String { 181â†’ val userAnswer = input 182â†’ val correctAnswer = correct 183â†’ Timber.d("correct answer = %s", correctAnswer) 184â†’ Timber.d("user answer = %s", userAnswer) 185â†’ return filterAnswer(answer, userAnswer, correctAnswer ?: "") 186â†’ } 187â†’ 188â†’ /** 189â†’ * Fill the placeholder for the type comparison. Show the correct answer, and the comparison if appropriate. 190â†’ * 191â†’ * @param answer The answer text 192â†’ * @param userAnswer Text typed by the user, or empty. 193â†’ * @param correctAnswer The correct answer, taken from the note. 194â†’ * @return The formatted answer text 195â†’ */ 196â†’ fun filterAnswer(answer: String, userAnswer: String, correctAnswer: String): String { 197â†’ val m: Matcher = PATTERN.matcher(answer) 198â†’ val sb = StringBuilder() 199â†’ fun append(@Language("HTML") html: String) = sb.append(html) 200â†’ 201â†’ var comparisonText = CollectionManager.compareAnswer(correctAnswer, userAnswer) 202â†’ if (doNotUseCodeFormatting) { 203â†’ comparisonText = "$comparisonText" 204â†’ } 205â†’ append(Matcher.quoteReplacement(comparisonText)) 206â†’ return m.replaceAll(sb.toString()) 207â†’ } 208â†’ 209â†’ companion object { 210â†’ /** Regular expression in card data for a 'type answer' after processing has occurred */ 211â†’ val PATTERN: Pattern = Pattern.compile("\\[\\[type:(.+?)]]") 212â†’ 213â†’ fun createInstance(preferences: SharedPreferences): TypeAnswer { 214â†’ return TypeAnswer( 215â†’ useInputTag = preferences.getBoolean("useInputTag", false), 216â†’ doNotUseCodeFormatting = preferences.getBoolean("noCodeFormatting", false), 217â†’ autoFocus = preferences.getBoolean("autoFocusTypeInAnswer", true) 218â†’ ) 219â†’ } 220â†’ 221â†’ /** 222â†’ * Return the correct answer to use for {{type::cloze::NN}} fields. 223â†’ * 224â†’ * @param txt The field text with the clozes 225â†’ * @param idx The index of the cloze to use 226â†’ * @return If the cloze strings are the same, return a single cloze string, otherwise, return 227â†’ * a string with a comma-separeted list of strings with the correct index. 228â†’ */ 229â†’ @VisibleForTesting 230â†’ fun contentForCloze(txt: String, idx: Int): String? { 231â†’ // In Android, } should be escaped 232â†’ val re = Pattern.compile("\\{\\{c$idx::(.+?)\\}\\}") 233â†’ val m = re.matcher(txt) 234â†’ val matches: MutableList = ArrayList() 235â†’ var groupOne: String 236â†’ while (m.find()) { 237â†’ groupOne = m.group(1)!! 238â†’ val colonColonIndex = groupOne.indexOf("::") 239â†’ if (colonColonIndex > -1) { 240â†’ // Cut out the hint. 241â†’ groupOne = groupOne.substring(0, colonColonIndex) 242â†’ } 243â†’ matches.add(groupOne) 244â†’ } 245â†’ val uniqMatches: Set = HashSet(matches) // Allow to check whether there are distinct strings 246â†’ 247â†’ // Make it consistent with the Desktop version (see issue #8229) 248â†’ return if (uniqMatches.size == 1) { 249â†’ matches[0] 250â†’ } else { 251â†’ matches.joinToString(", ") 252â†’ } 253â†’ } 254â†’ } 255â†’} 256â†’

1307â†’    @VisibleForTesting(otherwise = VisibleForTesting.PROTECTED)
1308â†’    open fun displayCardAnswer() {
1309â†’        // #7294 Required in case the animation end action does not fire:
1310â†’        actualHideEaseButtons()
1311â†’        Timber.d("displayCardAnswer()")
1312â†’        mediaErrorHandler.onCardSideChange()
1313â†’        backButtonPressedToReturn = false
1314â†’
1315â†’        // prevent answering (by e.g. gestures) before card is loaded
1316â†’        if (currentCard == null) {
1317â†’            return
1318â†’        }
1319â†’
1320â†’        // TODO needs testing: changing a card's model without flipping it back to the front
1321â†’        //  (such as editing a card, then editing the card template)
1322â†’        typeAnswer!!.updateInfo(getColUnsafe, currentCard!!, resources)
1323â†’
1324â†’        // Explicitly hide the soft keyboard. It *should* be hiding itself automatically,
1325â†’        // but sometimes failed to do so (e.g. if an OnKeyListener is attached).
1326â†’        if (typeAnswer!!.validForEditText()) {
1327â†’            val inputMethodManager = getSystemService(INPUT_METHOD_SERVICE) as InputMethodManager
1328â†’            inputMethodManager.hideSoftInputFromWindow(answerField!!.windowToken, 0)
1329â†’        }
1330â†’        displayAnswer = true
1331â†’        answerField!!.visibility = View.GONE
1332â†’        // Clean up the user answer and the correct answer
1333â†’        if (!typeAnswer!!.useInputTag) {
1334â†’            typeAnswer!!.input = answerField!!.text.toString()
1335â†’        }
1336â†’        isSelecting = false
1337â†’        val answerContent = htmlGenerator!!.generateHtml(getColUnsafe, currentCard!!, SingleCardSide.BACK)
1338â†’        automaticAnswer.onDisplayAnswer()
1339â†’        launchCatchingTask {
1340â†’            if (!automaticAnswerShouldWaitForAudio()) {
1341â†’                automaticAnswer.scheduleAutomaticDisplayQuestion()
1342â†’            }
1343â†’        }
1344â†’        updateCard(answerContent)
1345â†’        displayAnswerBottomBar()
1346â†’    }

1405â†’    /**
1406â†’     * Plays sounds (or TTS, if configured) for currently shown side of card.
1407â†’     *
1408â†’     * @param doAudioReplay indicates an anki desktop-like replay call is desired, whose behavior is identical to
1409â†’     * pressing the keyboard shortcut R on the desktop
1410â†’     */
1411â†’    @NeedsTest("audio is not played if opExecuted occurs when viewer is in the background")
1412â†’    protected open fun playSounds(doAudioReplay: Boolean) {
1413â†’        // this can occur due to OpChanges when the viewer is on another screen
1414â†’        if (!this.lifecycle.currentState.isAtLeast(RESUMED)) {
1415â†’            Timber.w("sounds are not played as the activity is inactive")
1416â†’            return
1417â†’        }
1418â†’        if (!soundPlayer.config.autoplay && !doAudioReplay) return
1419â†’        // Use TTS if TTS preference enabled and no other sound source
1420â†’        val useTTS = tts.enabled && !soundPlayer.hasSounds(displayAnswer)
1421â†’        // We need to play the sounds from the proper side of the card
1422â†’        if (!useTTS) {
1423â†’            launchCatchingTask {
1424â†’                val side = if (displayAnswer) SingleCardSide.BACK else SingleCardSide.FRONT
1425â†’                when (doAudioReplay) {
1426â†’                    true -> soundPlayer.replayAllSounds(side)
1427â†’                    false -> soundPlayer.playAllSounds(side)
1428â†’                }
1429â†’            }
1430â†’            return
1431â†’        }
1432â†’
1433â†’        val replayQuestion = soundPlayer.config.replayQuestion
1434â†’        // Text to speech is in effect here
1435â†’        // If the question is displayed or if the question should be replayed, read the question
1436â†’        if (ttsInitialized) {
1437â†’            if (!displayAnswer || doAudioReplay && replayQuestion) {
1438â†’                readCardTts(SingleCardSide.FRONT)
1439â†’            }
